description: Registry of known failure modes for transcript/slide/gap/working-paper systems. Used to seed review manifests and guide Claude review analysis.

failure_modes.title: Poor PDF Extraction
failure_modes.description: PDF-to-text conversion produces garbled, incomplete, or layout-corrupted text. Common with multi-column layouts, image-heavy slides, or scanned documents without OCR.
detection_signals: Extracted slide units have empty or single-character content fields
Entity names contain special characters or line-break artifacts
Claim confidence scores cluster near 0.0 for an entire slide deck
slide_intelligence_packet has zero extracted claims from a non-trivial slide deck
failure_modes.likely_downstream_impact: Gap detection operates on corrupted input, producing spurious gaps or missing real gaps. Working paper incorporates garbled claims without attribution errors.

failure_modes.title: False Alignment
failure_modes.description: A slide claim or transcript statement is incorrectly matched to a working paper section or gap category based on superficial keyword overlap rather than semantic equivalence.
detection_signals: Alignment confidence scores are high (>0.8) but aligned content is semantically unrelated
Multiple claims map to the same gap ID despite covering distinct topics
Traceability entries reference source documents that do not contain the attributed claim
failure_modes.likely_downstream_impact: Working paper makes incorrect assertions about consensus or evidence. Regulatory submissions cite misattributed evidence.

failure_modes.title: Decorative Gap Analysis
failure_modes.description: Gap detection produces output that is structurally valid but lacks substantive content. Gaps are listed as generic categories without specific evidence anchors.
detection_signals: All identified gaps have empty evidence arrays
Gap descriptions are template-like with no document-specific content
Readiness score is high despite absent or minimal evidence
Gap count is identical across multiple different transcripts
failure_modes.likely_downstream_impact: Working paper silently omits analysis depth. Review cycles miss real technical gaps. Regulatory arguments lack substantive backing.

failure_modes.title: Schema Valid but Semantically Empty
failure_modes.description: An output document passes JSON schema validation but contains placeholder content, empty arrays where data is expected, or template strings instead of actual analysis.
detection_signals: Key fields contain the string 'TODO' or 'N/A' in production output
Required list fields (key_findings, risks, decisions) are empty arrays
Numeric scores (confidence, readiness) are exactly 0.0 or 1.0 without explanation
Schema validation passes but downstream consumers raise KeyError or produce empty output
failure_modes.likely_downstream_impact: Silent degradation propagates through the pipeline. Consumers receive structurally valid but analytically worthless data.

failure_modes.title: Overmerged Conflict
failure_modes.description: Conflicting claims from different sources are silently merged into a single statement that misrepresents the nature of the disagreement.
detection_signals: Multiple source documents with contradictory values map to a single working paper claim
Conflict markers are absent in traceability appendix despite known disagreements
Patch generator applies conflicting patches without emitting a conflict flag
failure_modes.likely_downstream_impact: Working paper asserts false consensus. Reviewers and agencies receive a misleading summary of the technical state.

failure_modes.title: Missing Traceability
failure_modes.description: Claims, findings, or decisions in the working paper are not linked back to their originating source documents, transcripts, or slide signals.
detection_signals: source_traceability array in working paper appendix is empty or missing
Key findings list no supporting evidence references
Decisions and recommendations do not cite the meeting or document where they were established
failure_modes.likely_downstream_impact: Regulatory submissions cannot be audited. Review cycles cannot verify claims. Downstream systems cannot determine the provenance of assertions.

failure_modes.title: Silent Optional Input Fallback
failure_modes.description: A module silently falls back to stub or default behavior when an optional input is absent, without emitting a warning or marking the output as degraded.
detection_signals: Output contains stub sentinel fields (stub: true) in production artifacts
Pipeline completes successfully but output quality metrics are below baseline
Downstream consumer receives None or empty dict for an expected enrichment field
failure_modes.likely_downstream_impact: Pipeline appears healthy while producing degraded artifacts. Quality issues go undetected until manual review or downstream failure.

failure_modes.title: Brittle Frequency Extraction
failure_modes.description: Frequency values are extracted using fragile regex patterns that miss alternative notations (MHz vs GHz, ranges vs center frequencies, hyphenated vs en-dash ranges).
detection_signals: Frequency entity count is zero despite frequency-heavy input documents
Extracted frequencies are missing GHz values when only MHz regex is applied
Range notation '3550-3700 MHz' is extracted as two separate entities rather than one range
Frequency entities extracted from slide titles but not from body text
failure_modes.likely_downstream_impact: Gap detection misses spectrum-specific gaps. Working paper omits key frequency coordination data. Regulatory arguments are incomplete.
